name: 50 High-Fashion Editorial Prompts Vol.2
summary: Create bold, editorial-quality fashion imagery that commands attention and establishes premium brand positioning. 📷 Our meticulously crafted prompt book delivers 50 battle-tested prompts optimized for Gemini, Nano Banana, and Higgsfield, fully compatible with Character Reference tools for consistent, high-impact results. ⚡ Instant digital download.
description: This comprehensive Prompt Book contains 50 meticulously engineered AI prompts specifically designed to generate authentic, runway-ready high-fashion editorial photographs that capture bold aesthetics, creative direction, and magazine-cover sophistication.
why: Professional fashion editorial shoots cost $3,000–$8,000+ per session. Add designer wardrobe rentals ($500–$2,000), professional hair and makeup styling ($300–$800), location scouting and permits ($200–$1,000), lighting equipment and assistants ($500–$1,500), professional retouching ($200–$500 per image), and multiple shoot days—you're easily investing $8,000–$15,000+ for a single editorial campaign.And that's just for one aesthetic, one season, one collection. Want fresh fashion imagery? Want to test different concepts? Want to launch new designs? You're back to investing thousands—or months waiting for shoots to align with your timeline.But what if you could generate stunning, magazine-quality high-fashion editorial photos whenever you need them—instantly, for a fraction of the cost, with complete creative control? ✨
prompt: High-fashion beauty portrait of an elegant woman in a luxurious emerald satin gown and long matching gloves. She wears dazzling diamond jewelry — layered necklaces, chandelier earrings, and a large emerald ring. Her makeup is classic and glamorous: bold red lips, sharp eyeliner, flawless glowing skin, and sculpted cheekbones. Her sleek dark hair is styled straight and tucked behind the ears. The background is deep emerald green, creating a monochromatic, sophisticated atmosphere. Studio lighting with a soft spotlight, glossy editorial look inspired by Mert & Marcus and Vogue jewelry campaigns, ultra-detailed, no text, no watermark.
